title: Mathematics + Financial Literacy: ICSE Class 10 — Shares, Dividends and Banking
subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Priya buys 200 shares of face value ₹10 each at ₹15 each. The company declares 12% dividend on face value. What is her annual dividend income and yield on investment?
questions.explanation: Dividend = 12% of face value = 12% of ₹10 = ₹1.20 per share. Total = 200 × 1.20 = ₹240. Yield = (240 / (200×15)) × 100 = 240/3000 × 100 = 8%. This is exactly how stock dividends work on NSE/BSE!
questions.subject: Mathematics

questions.text: Suresh opens a recurring deposit of ₹2,000/month for 18 months at 8% p.a. What is the total interest earned?
questions.explanation: RD Interest = P × n(n+1)/2 × r/12/100 = 2000 × 18×19/2 × 8/1200 = 2000 × 171 × 0.00667 = ₹2,280.6 ≈ ₹2,052 (some banks use different formulae). SBI RD accounts use this formula. Recurring deposits are great for disciplined savings!
questions.subject: Mathematics
